name: marketing agencies
metaTitle: Contractor Management for Marketing Agencies | Axle
metaDescription: Run a marketing agency without spreadsheet chaos. Axle handles freelancer timesheets, retainer billing, OOO, and approvals — built for agency ops.
intro: Marketing agencies live and die by billable hours. When 60% of your delivery capacity is freelance — copywriters, designers, paid media specialists, video editors — your timesheet and invoice process is your margin. Axle gives agency ops a single place to track contractor hours per client, gate invoices behind supervisor approval, and pull clean utilization reports without piecing together five spreadsheets.
painPoints: Freelancers logging hours in five different formats every month
Retainer overruns nobody noticed until the client complained
Invoice approvals stuck in account-director email threads
No clean way to see contractor utilization across clients

useCases.title: Per-client utilization tracking
useCases.description: Tag every timesheet entry with a client or project so you can see exactly how many freelance hours a retainer is actually consuming — before you blow through the budget.

useCases.title: Account-director approval gates
useCases.description: Account directors approve their team's timesheets weekly; only approved hours unlock invoice submission. No more end-of-month reconciliation arguments.

useCases.title: Freelancer roster across services
useCases.description: Manage copywriters, designers, paid media buyers, and developers from one roster, with role-based permissions so each lead only sees their own people.

useCases.title: Cross-currency invoicing
useCases.description: Pay a US copywriter, an EU designer, and a LATAM developer from the same workflow — Axle records each invoice in its native currency.

faqs.q: How is Axle different from generic timesheet apps for marketing agencies?
faqs.a: Axle is built specifically for teams that work with independent contractors — not employees. The approval workflow gates invoices behind approved timesheets, every action is logged for audit, and the data model fits how marketing agencies actually engage and pay contractors.

faqs.q: Can marketing agencies run a global contractor team on Axle?
faqs.a: Yes. Axle records invoices in any currency, supports IBAN/SWIFT bank fields for international wires, and keeps a clean audit trail per contractor — which is exactly what cross-border teams need at month end.

faqs.q: Does Axle handle leave and OOO for contractors?
faqs.a: Yes. Contractors submit OOO requests, supervisors approve them, and approved OOO appears on a shared availability calendar. Hourly contractors don't bill for OOO days, and timesheet validation accounts for it automatically.

faqs.q: Is there a free plan that fits small marketing agencies?
faqs.a: Yes. Axle's free plan covers up to 3 contractors with full timesheet, invoice, and OOO functionality — plenty to get a small engagement off spreadsheets.

Engineering contractors on regulated systems
Risk and fraud specialists on retainer
Investor-ready and audit-ready record keeping

useCases.title: Role-based access
useCases.description: Compliance contractors only see what compliance contractors should see — no broader org-wide visibility unless explicitly granted.

useCases.title: Audit-grade records
useCases.description: Every approval and change logged with full attribution; survives regulatory inquiry and investor due diligence.

useCases.title: Tiered approvals
useCases.description: Layered approval workflows for sensitive engagements: tech lead, compliance, and finance can all be required to sign off.

useCases.title: Multi-currency engagement
useCases.description: Engage specialist contractors globally and keep clean records across currencies.

faqs.q: How is Axle different from generic timesheet apps for fintech companies?
faqs.a: Axle is built specifically for teams that work with independent contractors — not employees. The approval workflow gates invoices behind approved timesheets, every action is logged for audit, and the data model fits how fintech companies actually engage and pay contractors.

faqs.q: Can fintech companies run a global contractor team on Axle?
faqs.a: Yes. Axle records invoices in any currency, supports IBAN/SWIFT bank fields for international wires, and keeps a clean audit trail per contractor — which is exactly what cross-border teams need at month end.

faqs.q: Does Axle handle leave and OOO for contractors?
faqs.a: Yes. Contractors submit OOO requests, supervisors approve them, and approved OOO appears on a shared availability calendar. Hourly contractors don't bill for OOO days, and timesheet validation accounts for it automatically.

faqs.q: Is there a free plan that fits small fintech companies?
faqs.a: Yes. Axle's free plan covers up to 3 contractors with full timesheet, invoice, and OOO functionality — plenty to get a small engagement off spreadsheets.
